title: shuttleworthia tenera
intro: Shuttleworthia tenera, commonly known as the False Pimpernel, is a plant species belonging to the family Urticaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The therapeutic actions of Shuttleworthia tenera are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids and phenolic acids, that may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Further research is needed to fully understand the properties and effects of Shuttleworthia tenera, but initial findings suggest its potential as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", shuttleworthia tenera benefits include enhancing the immune system, specifically exhibiting immunostimulatory activities that can boost the body's ability to fight off infections.
side_effects: Shuttleworthia tenera can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high saponin content. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Shuttleworthia tenera may lead to kidney or liver damage, particularly when combined with other medications or substances. Additionally, improper use of the plant may cause allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, or difficulty breathing in sensitive individuals. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Shuttleworthia tenera or any other plant-based remedy to avoid potential side effects.
